scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: Skill explicitly states classifications are recommendations requiring QA confirmation; no direct regulatory determinations made.

methodological_ground.detail: Classification rules align with ICH E6(R2) and FDA 21 CFR Part 312 standards.

code_usability.detail: Script compiles cleanly; regex-based classification engine is runnable with no external dependencies.

functional_suitability.note: Core specialized research tooling functionality is complete; minor gaps in edge-case handling or output completeness exist.

reliability.note: Error handling is documented and the skill recovers gracefully from malformed inputs within specialized research tooling scope.

performance_context.note: Token usage is proportional to input complexity; execution overhead is acceptable for specialized research tooling tasks though room for compression exists.

agent_usability.note: Trigger description and step-level instructions are clear enough for downstream agent integration within specialized research tooling workflows.

human_usability.note: When-to-Use and When-Not-to-Use sections are clearly stated; error scenarios and recovery paths are documented for typical specialized research tooling use cases.

security.note: No credential exposure or injection vectors identified; input handling stays within specialized research tooling boundaries.

maintainability.note: The skill is modular and self-contained, with low overhead for future updates to specialized research tooling logic or output formats.

agent_specific.note: Alternative Classification now required in Output Requirements item 5 for confidence < 0.85; idempotency documented in Notes.

key_strengths: Alternative Classification now required in Output Requirements for borderline cases (confidence < 0.85)
Idempotency behavior documented in Notes with --id-prefix guidance for deterministic IDs
Comprehensive regex-based classification engine covering major, minor, and critical deviation categories
Strong regulatory grounding with specific ICH E6(R2), FDA 21 CFR, and EMA citations
Explicit QA confirmation disclaimer prevents misuse as a definitive regulatory tool

recommendations.title: Alternative classification not yet implemented in script code
recommendations.problem: Output Requirements now mandate alternative_classification for confidence < 0.85, but _apply_classification_rules still returns a single Classification enum without a secondary candidate.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md was updated but scripts/main.py ClassificationResult dataclass was not extended.
recommendations.fix: Add an 'alternative_classification' field to ClassificationResult. Populate it when confidence < 0.85 by returning the next-highest scoring classification from the rules engine.

recommendations.title: Composability not documented
recommendations.problem: The skill has clean JSON inputs/outputs but no documented integration points for use alongside other clinical trial skills.
recommendations.fix: Add a brief note: 'JSON output from batch mode can be consumed by downstream reporting or CAPA tracking skills.'
